name: Jordan
description_short: Jordan Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering auto loans, mortgages, certificates, credit cards, and deposit accounts with competitive rates.
description_long: Jordan Credit Union operates as a traditional credit union, providing comprehensive financial services to its membership base. The institution is structured as a member-owned cooperative, typical of credit unions, and maintains an online presence at jordancu.org with digital banking capabilities. According to their website, they focus on building "lasting financial relationships one member at a time," positioning themselves within the cooperative banking model rather than traditional commercial banking. The credit union has implemented modern digital infrastructure, including scheduled maintenance windows for their online banking platform and mobile applications, indicating active technology investment. Jordan Credit Union offers a broad array of consumer financial products spanning lending and deposit services. Their loan portfolio includes auto financing (new and used vehicles), mortgages (conventional fixed-rate), home equity lines of credit with introductory rates, certificates of deposit with tiered terms, and credit cards (Platinum and Platinum Plus Rewards Visa). Auto loans range from 4.74% APR for 60-month terms to 5.74% APR for 84-month terms, with additional RV, boat, and motorcycle financing options. Mortgage products feature 30-year conventional fixed rates starting at 6.0%, while home equity lines of credit offer 3.99% introductory rates before converting to 6.75% variable rates. Certificate accounts provide yields up to 4.05% APY for 36-month terms with $500 minimum balances. What distinguishes Jordan Credit Union is their emphasis on rapid loan application processing through a three-step online system with same-day approval callbacks, real-time transaction monitoring through their mobile app, and digital payment flexibility including remote check deposit and peer-to-peer transfers. Their marketing emphasizes convenience and accessibility, with multilingual support (English/Spanish) and comprehensive mobile banking features. However, their online presence provides limited information regarding membership eligibility requirements, specific fee structures, or detailed product terms, requiring members to contact branches directly for complete information. As a credit union, membership is a prerequisite for account access, which may be a limitation for consumers without qualifying affiliations. The website does not prominently display customer service contact information or branch locations beyond a "Locations" navigation link.
services: Auto and RV loans (new and used vehicles, 60-84 month terms)
Fixed-rate and adjustable-rate mortgages (30-year conventional loans)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) with introductory rates
Home equity fixed-rate loans (up to 84-month terms)
Credit cards (Platinum and Platinum Plus Rewards Visa)
Mobile banking app with transfers, bill payment, and remote check deposit
Real-time card transaction monitoring and spending limit controls
Online loan applications with rapid approval process
Online banking platform with scheduled maintenance windows
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.74% APR for 60-month terms
Certificate of deposit rates up to 4.05% APY for 36-month terms
Fast three-step online loan application process with same-day callback confirmation
Mobile app with real-time transaction monitoring, card spending limits, and remote check deposit
Home equity line of credit with 3.99% introductory rate for 6 months
Peer-to-peer payment functionality (pay-a-person transfers) integrated into mobile app
cons: Credit union membership eligibility requirements not clearly explained on website
Limited fee transparency—no visible fee schedules or product terms on homepage
Website lacks clear contact information and branch location details in main navigation
Introductory rate structure on HELOC (3.99% for 6 months, then 6.75% variable) creates payment increase risk
No information about rates for members with lower credit scores or alternative lending options
best_for: Credit union members seeking competitive auto financing with flexible term options
Homeowners evaluating mortgage refinancing or home equity borrowing with introductory rate incentives
Savers interested in certificate products with modest yield compared to broader market offerings
Consumers prioritizing mobile banking convenience and real-time account monitoring
diagnosis: Jordan Credit Union is best suited for existing or prospective members seeking traditional consumer banking services (auto loans, mortgages, deposits) with competitive rates and digital convenience. The primary limitation is that credit union membership is required for account access, and the website provides insufficient detail on eligibility criteria, fee structures, and product terms, necessitating direct member contact for complete information.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Jordan — Credit Union in South Jordan, UT | CreditDoc
meta_description: Jordan — NCUA-insured credit union in South Jordan, UT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
